prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_svr', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.3891) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.9528), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_svr, DWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (FT_svr, CWT_rf), suggesting horizon-specific weighting is valuable. Tier1 models dominate with 8 top performers while tier3 includes naive baselines. Given high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while downweighting poor performers.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_svr", "late": "CWT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ARIMA"],
  "tier2_models": ["THETA", "svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ETS", "CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.3891,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.3891) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.9528), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_svr, DWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (FT_svr, CWT_rf), suggesting horizon-specific weighting is valuable. Tier1 models dominate with 8 top performers while tier3 includes naive baselines. Given high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while downweighting poor performers."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k4"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=4 indicates horizon-specific model selection is valuable. relative_spread_mean=0.2857 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement favoring DBA and robust methods. RMSE spread between DWT_rf (3123.75) and NaiveSeasonal (4224.15) is ~36%, justifying top-k selection over full mean. With n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.9528). Top-4 candidates balance diversity: DBA for phase-shift robustness, best_per_horizon for horizon specialists, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 for weighted averaging with shrinkage, and topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 as a selection baseline."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.3891) and extremely high seasonality variance (0.9528), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_svr, DWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (FT_svr, CWT_rf), suggesting horizon-specific weighting is valuable. Tier1 models dominate with 8 top performers while tier3 includes naive baselines. Given high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while downweighting poor performers.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=4 indicates horizon-specific model selection is valuable. relative_spread_mean=0.2857 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement favoring DBA and robust methods. RMSE spread between DWT_rf (3123.75) and NaiveSeasonal (4224.15) is ~36%, justifying top-k selection over full mean. With n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.9528). Top-4 candidates balance diversity: DBA for phase-shift robustness, best_per_horizon for horizon specialists, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 for weighted averaging with shrinkage, and topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 as a selection baseline.
